title: Lane store, session→lane binding, lane-scoped feature-start (lib, RED-first)
action: Add per-feature lane records to the lib, additive over the default pipeline (per D4: zero-lane repos behave byte-identically). Lane record .bee/lanes/<feature>.json carries the same core as the default record: feature, mode, phase (closed vocabulary), approved_gates (all four), summary, next_action, plus created_at. readLane fail-open for display / readLaneStrict for mutation (mirror readState/readStateStrict discipline: corrupt file refuses loudly, file untouched). resolvePipeline(root, {sessionId}) returns the session's bound lane record when the session record names a lane that exists, else the default state.json view — resolution NEVER guesses and never scans; no sessionId or no binding = default. Session→lane binding lives on the S1 session record (a lane field; createSession's default OMITS the key when unbound, keeping the existing session-shape rows green) with a bind/unbind API in claims.mjs. startFeature gains an opt-in lane mode (start a feature AS a new lane while the default pipeline and other lanes stay untouched) with lane-scoped preconditions DEFINED AS (validated Q4 + cold-pickup repair — attribution is derived, no new fields invented): (a) nonterminal cells whose cell.feature equals the lane's feature block; (b) the global handoff record blocks a LANE start only when its feature field names this lane's feature (a handoff for another feature does not block; the DEFAULT start keeps today's any-handoff-blocks semantics); (c) a registered worker blocks the lane start only when its cell belongs to this lane's feature (worker→cell→feature derivation); (d) the global check: when the caller declares intended paths, any overlap with another session's active holds (claims/reservations) refuses. The default (non-lane) startFeature path keeps today's byte-identical semantics — its existing suite rows (grep startFeature in test_lib.mjs) MUST pass unmodified. Lane-start resets that lane's four gates in the same atomic write (spec R1 applied per lane). RED-first: failing rows before implementation, RED evidence in the trace. Vendor byte-identical siblings under .bee/bin/lib/.

truths: The pre-existing start-feature and state suite rows pass unmodified (zero-lane byte-parity)
resolvePipeline returns the default record when the session has no binding, and the bound lane record when it does; a binding to a missing lane resolves to a typed refusal, never a guess
A corrupt lane file: reads-for-display skip with a warning, mutations refuse loudly with the file untouched
Starting a lane resets exactly that lane's four gates and leaves the default record and every other lane byte-identical
A lane start is blocked by a handoff or worker only when that record derives to this lane's feature; unrelated handoffs/workers do not block it

artifacts.substantive: lane record CRUD + resolvePipeline + lane-mode startFeature with the derived lane-scoped preconditions and the global holds-overlap check

key_links: claims.mjs session records carry the lane binding resolvePipeline consumes
prohibitions: No edits to inject.mjs, guards.mjs, cells.mjs, bee.mjs, command-registry.mjs, or any hooks/ file (later cells own those)
No modification of existing start-feature/state test rows — new rows only
No new fields on HANDOFF.json or worker records (attribution is derived from their existing feature/cell fields)

trace.outcome: Lane store shipped additively (D4): per-feature lane records under .bee/lanes/<feature>.json with readLane (fail-open display, corrupt warns+skips) / readLaneStrict (corrupt refuses loudly, file untouched) / writeLane / removeLane / listLanes; resolvePipeline resolves session→bound-lane→default with typed LANE_INVALID/LANE_MISSING/LANE_CORRUPT refusals, never a guess; session→lane binding via bindSessionLane/unbindSessionLane on the S1 session record (lane key omitted while unbound); startFeature gains opt-in lane mode with the validated Q4 lane-scoped preconditions (same-feature nonterminal cells; handoff.feature attribution; worker→cell→feature derivation; declared-paths vs other-session active holds via pathsOverlap over claimed-cell files and reservations) resetting exactly that lane's four gates in one atomic write. Default startFeature path byte-identical; all 238 pre-existing rows pass unmodified; vendored siblings copied byte-identical.

deviations.what: Extended the EXPECTED_STATE_EXPORTS allowlist constant in templates/tests/test_lib.mjs with the 8 new lane exports (lanesDir, lanePath, readLane, readLaneStrict, writeLane, removeLane, listLanes, resolvePipeline).
deviations.why: The pre-existing export-surface row asserts EXACT-set equality of lib/state.mjs exports against that allowlist, so any deliberately added export turns it red by design. Its designed maintenance path is extending the allowlist data; the row's assertions are untouched. Recorded as a deviation because the cell prohibits modifying existing test rows — this is an extension of a row's fixture data (a list the row exists to have extended), not a modification of its checks.

trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": "Pinned start-feature/state rows (test_lib.mjs start-feature lib+CLI block, readState/readStateStrict rows, state-set byte-survival, worker prune), S1 session-shape rows (claims block — assert fields individually, so the omitted-when-unbound lane key stays invisible), export-surface exact-set row, vendored byte-parity sweep. C4 satisfied: full suite run green BEFORE the first edit (238 passed, 0 failed + onboard PASS) and after (250/0); pinned rows extended, never modified (one allowlist-data extension recorded as a deviation).",
  "tests_added_or_changed": "12 new lane rows in skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs (RED-first): lane CRUD round-trip incl. unicode/space names + path-shaped-name rejection; fail-open corrupt readLane/listLanes (warns, skips, file untouched); readLaneStrict loud refusal on corrupt + feature-mismatched records, missing→null; createSession omits lane key / bind / unbind removes the key / typed SESSION_MISSING / binding survives heartbeat; resolvePipeline default fallbacks (no sessionId, unknown session, unbound) + bound-lane resolution; typed LANE_MISSING/LANE_CORRUPT refusals naming the lane; lane-mode startFeature leaves state.json and every other lane byte-identical while default is mid-flight; same-feature nonterminal cells block, other features' claimed cells never do; HANDOFF feature attribution (lane) vs any-handoff-blocks (default) in one fixture; worker→cell→feature derivation blocks only this lane; declared-paths holds check (other-session claim-held cell files refuse naming holder, own session never blocked, reservation overlap refuses, expired hold passes, no paths = no check); terminal-lane restart resets four gates preserving created_at, mid-flight lane refuses, corrupt lane refuses untouched.",
  "red_failure_evidence": "Before implementation (tests committed first): suite exit=1, '238 passed, 12 failed' — exactly the 12 new rows RED: 'laneStore.writeLane is not a function', 'laneBinding.bindSessionLane is not a function', 'laneStore.resolvePipeline is not a function', and the lane-precondition rows failing against today's GLOBAL startFeature semantics (e.g. refusal citing 'current phase is \"swarming\"' instead of the same-feature cell; holds row: 'expected an error, none thrown'). Every pre-existing row stayed green during RED, proving the new rows — not drift — were the red.",
  "verification_run": "node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs → exit 0: '250 passed, 0 failed' and 'PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 1'. Includes the vendored byte-parity sweep over the copied .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs and .bee/bin/lib/claims.mjs siblings, and every pinned start-feature/state/session row unmodified (zero-lane byte-parity, D4).",
  "deliberate_exceptions": "None. The lane store is a brand-new surface only where no prior behavior existed (lane files); every touched pre-existing behavior (startFeature default path, session record shape, state.mjs export surface) is pinned by rows that ran green before and after."
}
trace.verify_output: Baseline BEFORE first edit (C4): 238 passed, 0 failed + onboard PASS. RED before implementation: exit=1, exactly the 12 new lane rows FAIL ('laneStore.writeLane is not a function', 'laneBinding.bindSessionLane is not a function', lane-scoped precondition assertions failing against today's global startFeature semantics), all 238 pre-existing rows PASS unmodified. GREEN after implementation + vendoring: exit=0, '250 passed, 0 failed' (238 pinned rows unmodified + 12 new lane rows) and onboard 'PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 1'.
